text.title: Help Users Recognise, Diagnose, Recover from Errors Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Help Users Recognise, Diagnose, Recover from Errors. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Error messages are in plain language; codes appear as a secondary detail, not the primary message.
rule.rationale: Plain language reaches the user; codes alone reach only support staff.

rule.statement: Message names what went wrong and points at the offending input or step.
rule.rationale: Generic 'invalid input' leaves users hunting for the cause.

rule.statement: Every error suggests a concrete next action — retry, edit field, contact support — never a dead end.
rule.rationale: Dead-end errors push users into rage-clicks or abandonment.

rule.statement: On error, the user input is preserved; the form is not cleared.
rule.rationale: Clearing input on error is a recurring high-severity complaint.

rule.statement: Each error code is instrumented so support can correlate volume and severity.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-error-recovery.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
